source_grounding.caveat: Clean-room product blueprint inspired by the public Jira + Confluence pairing. Not an Atlassian private-implementation claim, not a clone, and not a trademarked product.

selected_product_grounding.catalog_role: Primary work graph and workflow engine.
Knowledge graph and durable decision memory.

product_thesis.one_liner: An open-source work-and-knowledge operating system: a work graph plus a knowledge graph that share identity, permissions, search, and audit, designed to be self-hostable and AI-ready without cloning any vendor product.
target_users: Small-to-mid engineering and product teams that want self-hostable work tracking plus durable documents under one identity and permission model.
OSS maintainers and platform teams that need a transparent reference implementation of a system-of-work surface, with API-first contracts and clear governance.
Operators who need to deploy, monitor, back up, and audit the system without proprietary lock-in.
non_goals: Cloning Jira, Confluence, or any other vendor product surface, branding, schema, or private APIs.
Reimplementing every Atlassian feature; the scope is the two graphs and the integration surface between them.
Multi-cloud and enterprise-portfolio features before the single-cloud, single-tenant production posture is stable.
Building an LLM/agent platform before the deterministic data, API, permission, and audit layers are sound.
clean_room_rules: Use the public Atlassian software-homepage product catalog only for inspiration about responsibilities, not for any private architecture claims.
Do not invent Atlassian-internal services, schemas, team names, or metrics.
Avoid trademarked product names, logos, and slogans in the application surface and documentation.

phases.name: Idea and public-source grounding
phases.objective: Lock the product thesis to public, repo-grounded sources and define what is in scope and what is explicitly out of scope.
jira_inspired_requirements: Confirm Jira's catalog role as the primary work graph and workflow engine before specifying any work-graph requirement.
confluence_inspired_requirements: Confirm Confluence's catalog role as the knowledge graph and durable decision memory before specifying any document/knowledge requirement.
cross_product_integration_requirements: Document the integration surface as bidirectional linking between work items and pages, with shared identity, permissions, search, and audit; do not invent additional cross-product surfaces.
acceptance_gates: Selected products are exactly jira and confluence in the catalog file.
Every claim about Atlassian inspiration cites an existing repo source.
No trademarked product clone language appears in the deliverables.
anti_hallucination_checks: Reject any product reference outside selected_products plus allowed_platform_capabilities.
Reject any architectural claim that names an Atlassian-internal service, schema, or team.
If evidence is insufficient for a statement, write 'not supported by current public sources' instead of guessing.

kanban_child_task_shape.title: phase 0: PM product selection and production OSS phase specification

exit_criteria: Grand-design doc and phase catalog exist with the same selected products and phase ids.
Phase catalog passes structural validation tests added in phase 4 work.
All anti-hallucination checks for phase 0 pass.

phases.name: Product thesis, target users, and non-goals
phases.objective: Define the open-source product as a clean-room work-and-knowledge operating system: who it is for, what it is not, and what success looks like.
jira_inspired_requirements: Capture work-graph responsibilities at the thesis level: trackable work items, workflow states, ownership, links to knowledge.
confluence_inspired_requirements: Capture knowledge-graph responsibilities at the thesis level: durable documents, structured spaces, decisions, and links back to work.
cross_product_integration_requirements: State that the product treats the work graph and the knowledge graph as first-class peers under one identity, permission, and audit model.
acceptance_gates: Target users list is concrete and bounded.
Non-goals list explicitly forbids cloning vendor products.
Thesis avoids vendor branding.
anti_hallucination_checks: No fictitious user research, customer names, or metrics.
No invented enterprise commitments such as uptime numbers or compliance certificates.
exit_criteria: Thesis is one paragraph and three lists (users, non-goals, clean-room rules).
Reviewer confirms no vendor-clone language and no invented facts.

phases.name: Domain model and information architecture
phases.objective: Model the minimum durable domain that supports work items and knowledge pages under shared identity and permissions.
jira_inspired_requirements: Work item entity with id, type, title, description, status, assignee, reporter, owning team, parent/child links, and timestamps.
Workflow with states and allowed transitions, owned per project/space.
Project entity that owns work items, with permissions and an audit boundary.
confluence_inspired_requirements: Page entity with id, title, body, author, owning team/space, version history, and immutable revisions.
Space entity that owns pages, with permissions and an audit boundary.
Comment entity attached to pages and work items with stable ids.
cross_product_integration_requirements: Bidirectional link entity between work items and pages with link type and direction.
Single user, group, and team model shared by work and knowledge.
build_artifacts: Pydantic/JSON-schema models referenced from docs (planned for engineering phase).
acceptance_gates: Every domain entity declares owner team and audit boundary.
No entity references an undefined platform capability.
Cross-graph links are explicit and versionable.
anti_hallucination_checks: Do not invent Atlassian-private schema fields.
Do not invent platform capabilities outside allowed_platform_capabilities.
exit_criteria: Domain entities cover both graphs and their integration links.
Entities map to allowed_platform_capabilities only.

phases.name: API contract and permission model
phases.objective: Specify the API surface and permission model so engineering can implement deterministically and operators can reason about access control.
jira_inspired_requirements: REST endpoints for projects, work items, transitions, links, comments, and search.
confluence_inspired_requirements: REST endpoints for spaces, pages, versions, comments, and search.
Page version conflict handling and history retrieval.
cross_product_integration_requirements: Shared OAuth2/OIDC identity, shared RBAC model with project/space scope, and a shared audit endpoint.
Cross-graph link endpoints that require permission on both ends.
acceptance_gates: All mutating endpoints require auth.
All endpoints document the permission scope required.
All endpoints emit an audit event.
anti_hallucination_checks: Do not reference Atlassian-private endpoints or response shapes.
Do not invent OAuth scopes that are not defined locally.
exit_criteria: OpenAPI extension validates locally.
Permission model is explicit per endpoint.
Every endpoint has an audit-event contract.

phases.name: MVP vertical slice
phases.objective: Build a runnable, testable end-to-end slice: create a work item, create a page, link them, list them filtered by permission.
jira_inspired_requirements: Create, read, update, transition, and link a work item via API.
confluence_inspired_requirements: Create, read, version-update, and link a page via API.
cross_product_integration_requirements: Bidirectional link with permission check on both endpoints.
Search endpoint that filters out objects the caller cannot read.
build_artifacts: Reference implementation in src/innerwork/ (planned future work).
acceptance_gates: Happy path passes integration tests.
Permission-denied paths are covered by tests.
Audit events are written for every mutation.
anti_hallucination_checks: No mock data that pretends to be production data.
No claims of feature parity with Jira or Confluence.
exit_criteria: MVP slice runs from a clean checkout with documented commands.
Integration tests cover happy path and permission denial.

phases.name: Collaboration, notifications, import/export
phases.objective: Add the collaboration surface needed for real teams: comments, mentions, notifications, and structured import/export.
jira_inspired_requirements: Comment thread on work items with @mentions.
Notification on assignment, transition, and mention.
confluence_inspired_requirements: Comment thread on pages with @mentions and inline comments-by-anchor where the renderer supports it.
cross_product_integration_requirements: Cross-graph mention resolution that points to the same user across work items and pages.
JSON-based import/export schema for projects, spaces, work items, pages, comments, and links.
acceptance_gates: Notifications respect user preferences and quiet hours where defined.
Import/export is round-trippable for a small fixture.
anti_hallucination_checks: Do not invent email-template content claimed to be vendor-standard.
Do not claim third-party connectors that are not actually implemented.
exit_criteria: Import/export round-trip test passes.
Notification dispatch is observable and rate-limited.

phases.name: Search, analytics, and AI-ready context boundaries
phases.objective: Make the system queryable, measurable, and safe to expose to AI assistants via explicit context boundaries.
jira_inspired_requirements: Indexed search over work items respecting permissions.
Basic analytics: open/in-progress/done counts, cycle time per project.
confluence_inspired_requirements: Indexed search over pages and comments respecting permissions.
Basic analytics: edit recency, contributor counts per space.
cross_product_integration_requirements: Unified search endpoint that joins work items and pages.
Explicit AI context-boundary contract: only objects the caller can read are returned, with a token budget and a redaction list.
build_artifacts: Docs section on AI-ready context boundaries.
acceptance_gates: Permission-respecting search test passes.
AI context-boundary contract is documented and tested for redaction.
anti_hallucination_checks: Do not claim to use a vendor LLM that is not actually wired in.
Do not assert benchmark numbers without a reproducible script.

kanban_child_task_shape.title: phase 6: search analytics and AI boundaries

exit_criteria: Search returns only permitted objects.
AI context endpoint redacts according to documented rules.

phases.name: Reliability, security, privacy, and compliance hardening
phases.objective: Bring the system to a baseline production-trust posture: backups, secret management, threat model, privacy controls, and audit completeness.
confluence_inspired_requirements: Audit every page version write and visibility change.
cross_product_integration_requirements: Shared audit pipeline.
Threat model that explicitly covers cross-graph linking abuse and mention scraping.
acceptance_gates: Threat model lists assets, actors, vectors, and mitigations.
Backups are tested by restoring into a clean environment.
Privacy-relevant fields are documented.
anti_hallucination_checks: Do not assert compliance certifications that have not been issued.
Do not name auditors or attesters that have not engaged with the project.
exit_criteria: Threat model is reviewed and accepted by an independent reviewer.
Backup/restore drill passes.

phases.name: Deployment, operations, SLOs, observability, release engineering
phases.objective: Make the system deployable, observable, and operable in production with explicit SLOs and release discipline.
jira_inspired_requirements: Operational dashboards for workflow throughput and error rates.
confluence_inspired_requirements: Operational dashboards for page-write success and version-conflict rate.
cross_product_integration_requirements: Unified deployment topology (broker, workers, store, search, optional sidecars).
Release engineering with signed artifacts, change log, and rollback plan.
acceptance_gates: SLO targets are documented per critical endpoint.
Release rollback procedure is executable and rehearsed.
Observability covers logs, metrics, and traces for both graphs.
anti_hallucination_checks: Do not claim achieved SLO numbers in absence of measurement.
Do not claim CDN/edge providers that are not integrated.
exit_criteria: Staging deployment is reproducible from CI.
Rollback drill passes.

phases.name: Open-source governance, contributor experience, packaging, docs
phases.objective: Make the project legitimately community-runnable: license, code of conduct, contributor guide, release cadence, packaging, and docs site.
jira_inspired_requirements: Contributor docs describe how to add a new work-item type without forking core.
confluence_inspired_requirements: Contributor docs describe how to add a new page macro/renderer plug-in without forking core.
cross_product_integration_requirements: Single contributor guide covering both graphs.
Governance describes how breaking-change decisions are made across the two graphs.
build_artifacts: Release packaging via pyproject.toml and tagged releases.
acceptance_gates: License, CoC, security policy, and contributor guide exist and are linked from README.
Release packaging produces a versioned artifact that is installable from a tag.
anti_hallucination_checks: Do not list maintainers who have not opted in.
Do not claim foundation affiliation that has not been granted.
exit_criteria: External contributor can land a non-trivial PR using only documented workflow.
A tagged release is installable from PyPI or equivalent.

phases.name: Beta, migration, launch, and post-launch iteration
phases.objective: Drive the project from internal readiness through a public beta and into ongoing iteration with measurable feedback loops.
jira_inspired_requirements: Public beta surfaces work-item lifecycles; bugs are tracked in the same instance as a dogfooding signal.
confluence_inspired_requirements: Public beta surfaces page editing and version history; release notes are written in the system itself.
cross_product_integration_requirements: Migration guide for importing from common JSON exports.
Public roadmap that links phases 0-10 to ongoing issues.
acceptance_gates: Beta program has documented onboarding and offboarding.
Migration path is tested with at least one synthetic fixture.
Post-launch iteration loop is documented and operating.
anti_hallucination_checks: Do not claim beta user counts without published evidence.
Do not assert revenue, pricing, or commercial relationships.
exit_criteria: Beta has run for a documented window.
At least one external migration has been completed end-to-end.
Post-launch iteration cadence is in place.

active_roadmap.product_scope: Innerwork: Jira/Confluence-inspired work graph plus knowledge graph with shared identity, permissions, search, links, and audit. Other Atlassian portfolio products remain catalog context only.

phases.name: Dockerized platform PoC
phases.objective: Run the current FastAPI backend in Docker with local SQLite persistence and a visible API shell.
phases.frontend: Server-rendered landing page plus generated API docs; no dedicated product UI yet.
phases.backend: FastAPI broker, idempotency, SQLite state, policy profiles, deterministic control-plane snapshots.
exit_criteria: docker compose up --build starts the service
/healthz, /docs, and /openapi.json are reachable
demo service intent persists across restart

phases.name: Work-and-knowledge MVP
phases.objective: Implement projects, work items, workflow states, spaces, pages, versions, comments, and links.
phases.frontend: May still use API docs or a minimal shell; real product UI waits for Phase C.
phases.backend: Add product-domain APIs and SQLite persistence for both graphs.
exit_criteria: create project/work item/space/page/link from Docker demo
restart preserves MVP entities

phases.name: Product frontend
phases.objective: Build a real browser UI for work items, pages, links, and search.
phases.frontend: Dedicated product UI replaces Swagger as the primary demo surface.
exit_criteria: user completes MVP flow without curl or Swagger UI
no vendor branding or trade dress

phases.name: Permission, identity, and audit
phases.objective: Add local authn/authz, scoped permissions, and append-only audit events.
phases.backend: Users, groups, teams, project/space scopes, audit stream.
exit_criteria: permission-denied paths tested
all mutations emit audit events

phases.name: Durable worker and operations
phases.objective: Add async workers, retries, replay, backups, and drift/reconciliation workflows.
phases.frontend: Operator status surfaces for operation history and replay.
phases.backend: Queue abstraction, retry/backoff, poison queue, backup/restore.
exit_criteria: worker crash resumes safely
backup restores into clean Docker environment

phases.name: Search, collaboration, import/export
phases.objective: Add permissioned search, collaboration features, and portable import/export.
phases.backend: Search index abstraction, notification dispatcher, import/export schema.
exit_criteria: search hides unauthorized objects
import/export round trip passes

phases.name: AI-ready context boundary
phases.objective: Expose permission-filtered, redacted context endpoints for assistants without depending on a vendor LLM.
phases.backend: Context API with provenance, token budget, and redaction.
exit_criteria: unauthorized objects excluded
redaction and provenance tested

phases.name: Production deployment and observability
phases.objective: Add Postgres, migrations, observability, release artifacts, SLOs, and rollback drills.
phases.backend: Postgres mode, migrations, metrics, traces, release workflow.
exit_criteria: staging deployment reproducible
rollback drill passes

phases.name: OSS governance and extension model
phases.objective: Make the project community-runnable with governance, security policy, extension points, docs, and releases.
exit_criteria: external contributor can land documented PR
tagged release installs cleanly

phases.name: Beta and migration loop
phases.objective: Run beta, test migrations, dogfood the app, and publish iteration cadence.
exit_criteria: synthetic migration completes end-to-end
beta feedback triaged through Innerwork
